title: PostgreSQL 18 Domain Schema, Keys, Constraints, and Index Intent · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent community archive receives a different publisher mix, malformed feed, validator state, tenant, schedule, database condition, accessibility need, platform, and injected failure and transfers postgresql 18 domain schema, keys, constraints, and index intent into a changed constrained relational feed-service schema. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of constrained relational feed-service schema with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress thr…
objectives: Model tenants, users, sources, feeds, subscriptions, entries, authors, categories, content versions, fetches, and reader state with clear ownership.
Use stable internal keys plus scoped external identities and preserve canonical URL changes without breaking references.
Encode nullability, checks, unique scopes, foreign keys, cascades, timestamps, states, and length budgets in PostgreSQL.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Go Relational Entities
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go team handles case 5716 during an accessible community-news reader review. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile feed-service entities, database invariants, tenant keys, and query-driven indexes; every goroutine, body, row set, transaction, lease, timer, and pool needs named cleanup ownership; then change one schema, constraint, transaction, upsert, migration, query, or search plan and preserve admitted state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Go Relational Entities.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for model tenants, users, sources, feeds, subscriptions, entries, authors, categories, content versions, fetches, and reader state with clear ownership. For Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Go Relational Entities, keep Feed Go Relational Entities connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: feed-go-tenant-schema-isolation-wwewwk" that uses case 4049 to reconcile feed-service entities, database invariants, tenant keys, and query-driven indexes. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, parse through native XML libraries, contact publishers, access PostgreSQL, read host files, handle real signals, launch unbounded goroutines, or claim race, load, accessibility, container, backup, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, pgx 5.10.0, goose 3.27.2, PostgreSQL 18.4, original fixtures, controlled RoundTrippers and httptest servers, disposable databases, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected images, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, and release gates.

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Feed Go Tenant Schema Isolation.

Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile feed-service entities, database invariants, tenant keys, and query-driven indexes; browser Go must remain deterministic and free of network, database, process, and host effects; then change one RSS, Atom, namespace, date, identity, content, or encoding case and reconcile provenance. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
